title: विष्णु पञ्चरत्नम्
devanagariText:
॥ श्री विष्णु पञ्चरत्नम् ॥

नारायणं परमब्रह्म सर्वकारणकारणम्।
प्रपद्ये पुण्डरीकाक्षं शान्ताकारं भुजङ्गशम्॥ १॥

पद्मनाभं सुरेशानं विश्वाधारं गगनसद्ृशम्।
मेघवर्णं शुभाङ्गं च लक्ष्मीकान्तं नमाम्यहम्॥ २॥

मत्स्यादिदशरूपेण जगत्पालनमादधे।
आद्यन्तरहितं विष्णुं सर्वव्यापिनमाश्रये॥ ३॥

शङ्खचक्रधरं देवं गरुडध्वजमच्युतम्।
वैकुण्ठवासिनं शान्तं जगदीशं नमाम्यहम्॥ ४॥

सर्वधर्ममयं विष्णुं सर्वदेवनमस्कृतम्।
सर्वभूतात्मभूतस्थं सर्वेशं प्रणमाम्यहम्॥ ५॥

विष्णोः पञ्चरत्नमिदं यः पठेद्भक्तिसंयुतः।
सर्वपापविनिर्मुक्तो वैकुण्ठगतिमाप्नुयात्॥

॥ इति श्री विष्णु पञ्चरत्नम् सम्पूर्णम् ॥
transliteration:
|| Shri Vishnu Pancharatnam ||

Narayanam Paramabrahma Sarvakaranakaranam |
Prapadye Pundarikaksham Shantakaram Bhujangasham || 1 ||

[...]

|| Iti Shri Vishnu Pancharatnam Sampurnam ||
hindiMeaning:
नारायण, परमब्रह्म, सर्वकारणकारण, पुण्डरीकाक्ष, शान्ताकार की शरण।

पद्मनाभ, मेघवर्ण, लक्ष्मीकान्त को नमन। दशावतारधारी विष्णु की शरण।

शंखचक्रधर, गरुडध्वज, अच्युत, वैकुण्ठवासी जगदीश को प्रणाम।
benefits: Five jewel-verses of Lord Vishnu the Preserver
Invokes Vishnu as Param Brahma and cosmic cause
Invokes vaikuntha gati and freedom from sins
Compact yet powerful daily Vishnu prayer
Ideal for Ekadashi, Thursday, and Vishnu festivals
description: Vishnu Pancharatnam — विष्णु पञ्चरत्नम् — is five gems of devotional verses offered to Lord Vishnu. Rooted in Adi Shankaracharya, this composition carries deep spiritual significance. Chanting this stotra with devotion is said to invoke Vishnu as Param Brahma and cosmic cause as well as compact yet powerful daily Vishnu prayer. Additionally, it is known to grant Vaikuntha gati and freedom from sins. This sacred text is ideally recited on Thursday (Guruvar) and during Devuthani Ekadashi for enhanced blessings. The text contains 5 verses and takes around 2 minutes to recite, fitting conveniently into a daily spiritual routine. On this page, you can read the complete Vishnu Pancharatnam in Devanagari Sanskrit with English transliteration, Hindi meaning (arth), and free PDF download for offline recitation.
seoDescription: Read Vishnu Pancharatnam in Sanskrit with Hindi meaning. Five jewel-verses of Lord Vishnu for devotion and Vaikuntha gati.

seoFaqs.question: What is the Vishnu Pancharatnam and who composed it?
seoFaqs.answer: The Vishnu Pancharatnam (विष्णु पञ्चरत्नम्) is a beloved five-verse devotional stotra of Lord Vishnu, traditionally attributed to Sri Adi Shankaracharya as part of his rich Pancharatnam compositions in praise of various deities. The name Pancharatnam means five jewels (pancha — five, ratna — jewel), each verse a precious meditation on a different aspect of Lord Vishnu — as Para-Brahman (verse 1), as Lakshmi-kanta (verse 2), as the Dasha-avatara who sustains creation (verse 3), as Vaikuntha-vasi the discus-and-conch bearer (verse 4), and as the Sarveshvara who is the indwelling Self of all beings (verse 5). It is concise, accessible, and deeply theological — perfect for daily recitation. (Source: Stotra Sahitya of Adi Shankaracharya.)

seoFaqs.question: What is the meaning of the opening verse Narayanam Param Brahma?
seoFaqs.answer: The opening jewel of the Vishnu Pancharatnam declares: Narayanam Param Brahma Sarva-karana-karanam, Prapadye Pundarikaksham Shantakaram Bhujanga-sham — meaning: I take refuge in (prapadye) Narayana, the supreme Brahman (Param Brahma), the cause of all causes (sarva-karana-karanam), the lotus-eyed (pundarikaksha), the embodiment of peace (shantakaram), reposing on the serpent Shesha (bhujanga-sham). In a single verse, Shankara has compressed the entire Vaishnava-Vedanta theology — Narayana is identified with the Para-Brahman of the Upanishads, with the cosmic First Cause, with the lotus-eyed personal Lord of bhakti, and with the cosmic image of peace upon the serpent of infinity. (Source: Vishnu Pancharatnam verse 1.)

seoFaqs.question: How does the Vishnu Pancharatnam describe the Dasha-avatara?
seoFaqs.answer: Verse 3 of the Vishnu Pancharatnam beautifully captures the Vaishnava theology of avatara: Matsyadi-dasha-rupena jagat-palanam-adadhe, Adyantarahitam Vishnum sarvavyapinam-aashraye — meaning: I take refuge in Vishnu, the all-pervading, beginning-less and end-less, who undertakes the protection of the world (jagat-palanam) by descending in the ten forms beginning with Matsya (the fish). The ten avataras — Matsya, Kurma, Varaha, Narasimha, Vamana, Parashurama, Rama, Krishna, Buddha, Kalki — are not separate gods but expressions of the same eternal Vishnu, each appearing at the right moment of cosmic need. The verse harmonises the avatara theology of bhakti with the formless Brahman theology of Advaita. (Source: Vishnu Pancharatnam verse 3; Bhagavata Purana avatara theology.)

seoFaqs.question: What benefits does Vishnu Pancharatnam grant according to its phalashruti?
seoFaqs.answer: The phalashruti is brief but supremely powerful: Vishnoh pancharatnam-idam yah pathed-bhakti-samyutah, Sarva-papa-vinirmukto Vaikuntha-gatim-aapnuyat — meaning: he who recites this Vishnu Pancharatnam endowed with bhakti is liberated from all sins and attains the gati (path / destination) of Vaikuntha, the eternal abode of Vishnu. The two key promises are sarva-papa-vinirmukti (complete freedom from sins, even those accumulated over many lives) and Vaikuntha-prapti (the supreme Vaishnava moksha — eternal residence in Vishnu's transcendental world). For such a short composition (5 verses, 2 minutes of recitation), the promised fruit is extraordinarily profound — possible because of the densely concentrated theological power of each verse. (Source: Vishnu Pancharatnam phalashruti.)

seoFaqs.question: When should the Vishnu Pancharatnam be recited?
seoFaqs.answer: The Vishnu Pancharatnam is most powerful on Thursday (Guruvar — Vishnu's day, sacred also to Brihaspati), every Ekadashi (the eleventh lunar day sacred to Vishnu — fasted by Vaishnavas worldwide), Dvadashi (the day of breaking Ekadashi fast with Vishnu darshan), Vaikuntha Ekadashi (Margashirsha-Pushya Shukla Ekadashi — the supreme festival when the Vaikuntha Dwara opens at Vishnu temples like Tirupati and Srirangam), Devshayani Ekadashi (Ashadha Shukla — Vishnu enters yoga-nidra), Devuthani / Prabodhini Ekadashi (Karthik Shukla — Vishnu wakes), every major Vishnu festival (Rama Navami, Krishna Janmashtami, Narasimha Jayanti, Vamana Jayanti), and as a daily morning nityapaath. (Sources: Vaishnava Ekadashi paddhati; Drikpanchang.)

seoFaqs.question: How is the Vishnu Pancharatnam different from the Vishnu Dwadash Nama?
seoFaqs.answer: Both are short Vishnu prayers but with different focus and style. The Vishnu Dwadash Nama Stotram (taught by Sage Narada, preserved in Padma Purana) is a litany-stotra — it enumerates the twelve sacred names of Vishnu (Keshava through Damodara) for nama-japa and sankalpa use, and is the foundational entry-level Vishnu prayer recited at every ritual. The Vishnu Pancharatnam (composed by Shankara) is a meditation-stotra in five jewel-verses — each verse contemplates Vishnu under a different theological aspect (Param Brahma, Lakshmi-kanta, Dasha-avatara, Vaikuntha-vasi, Antaryamin), and is more philosophical in tone. The Dwadash Nama is for nama-smaranam; the Pancharatnam is for tattva-dhyana. Both are recited daily by serious Vaishnavas. (Sources: Padma Purana; Stotra Sahitya of Shankara.)
